checks.detail: Baseline emits empty string and matches only 1/385 expected outputs (~0.0026 correctness, ~0.0013 gated perf, composite ~0.002 << 0.01).
Correctness is byte-equal vs CommonMark reference HTML and perf is wall-time vs cmark with 5x slack, so any valid C inline implementation scores equally.
Performance is gated by correctness (good), but correctness is NOT gated by performance under arithmetic-mean aggregation, so a correct-but-slow solution still scores 0.5 with zero perf.
reasons: weighted_sum allows a fully-correct but arbitrarily slow converter to harvest the entire 0.5 correctness weight while contributing 0 to perf
consider multiplicative aggregation or power-mean(p<0) so weak perf also drags the total down, symmetric with the existing correctness->perf gate

checks.detail: Empty/constant/copy-input outputs fail byte-equal grading on ~all 385 cases, and static + execve tripwires block external Markdown libraries and shell-outs to cmark/pandoc/python.
compute_reward.py runs outside strace as root, takes only CLI counters, and never imports/reads /app, so it is independently re-runnable from the staged manifests and counters.
Both fail_with and the success path write reward.json with score (float), subscores (list of {subtask,score}), and additional_data (dict); failure path includes subscores with zeros.

reward_design.formula: composite = 0.5 * correctness_rate + 0.5 * (perf_raw_rate * correctness_rate); correctness_rate = passed / (visible_total + hidden_total); perf_raw_rate = mean over perf files of min(1.0, cmark_median * 5 / agent_median)
reward_design.formula_explanation: Equal-weighted correctness and performance, with performance multiplicatively gated by correctness so a fast-but-wrong binary collapses to ~0 instead of harvesting the perf half.

components.name: correctness
components.metric: byte-equal match against CommonMark reference HTML across 268 visible + 117 hidden inline cases

components.name: performance
components.metric: per-file min(1, cmark_median*5/agent_median) averaged over perf files (currently 1 file)

dimension_analysis.name: correctness
dimension_analysis.notes: 385 strict byte-equal CommonMark inline cases including emphasis precedence, multiple-of-3 rule, link destinations, entity refs, code span normalization.

dimension_analysis.name: performance
dimension_analysis.notes: 5x slack vs cmark on perf_links.md is achievable for any reasonable C implementation; gated by correctness so cannot be gamed by a fast no-op.

notes: Consider multiplicative aggregation (composite = correctness * (a + b*perf_raw)) or power-mean(p<0) so a correct-but-very-slow solution does not still earn the full 0.5 correctness weight; current gate is one-directional.
Only 1 perf file (perf_links.md) — add a few more covering emphasis-heavy, escape-heavy, and large-paragraph inputs to make the perf signal less brittle to a single workload shape.
